content.problemStatement:
Today is chef's friend's birthday. He wants to give a gift to his friend. So he was desperately searching for some gift here and there. 



Fortunately, he found an array a of size **n** lying around. The array contains positive integers. Chef's friend likes even numbers very much. So for the gift, chef will choose a ** consecutive non-empty** segment of the array. The segment should contain exactly **k** even integers. Though it can have any number of odd integers.
He will then pick that segment and gift it to his friend. 



But there is a problem. It might not be always possible for the chef to choose such a segment. Please tell whether it is possible for chef to select some gift or not?
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains two integers N and K on the first line, followed by N integers on the second line.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print YES if it is possible to select a segment with exactly K even integers, otherwise print NO.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
For  first  test case, we can select a[1, 1] = {2}. 
For second  test case, we can select a[1, 2] = {2, 6}. 
For third  test case, we can not select any consecutive segment having exactly 3 even numbers. 
For fourth  test case, we can select a[0, 1] = {1, 2} or a[1, 2] = {2, 4}.
We can select any segment with no even integers, for example a[0, 0] = {1}.

hidden.label: stress/general
hidden.expectedOutput:
YES
YES
NO
YES
YES
YES

solution.approach: The problem can be solved by iterating over all possible segments of the array and checking if any segment contains exactly K even integers. This can be achieved by using two nested loops to generate all possible segments and then counting the number of even integers in each segment.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N^2) per test case, O(T * N^2) total.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — no additional space is used.
